indicators.name: Breaks down problems
indicators.description: Splits a complex problem into smaller, manageable sub-problems.

indicators.name: Identifies patterns
indicators.description: Recognizes similarities or repeating structures across problems.

indicators.name: Designs step-by-step solutions
indicators.description: Sequences logical steps (an algorithm) needed to reach a solution.

indicators.name: Evaluates solution efficiency
indicators.description: Considers whether a solution is clear, complete, and reasonably efficient.

name: 2. Programming & Implementation Skills
description: The learner can translate logical solutions into functional code using appropriate programming constructs such as variables, conditionals, loops, and functions. They demonstrate the ability to write clear, organized code, test outputs, identify errors, and apply basic debugging techniques to improve program correctness and efficiency.

indicators.name: Uses core programming constructs
indicators.description: Applies variables, conditionals, loops, and functions correctly to build a working program.

indicators.name: Writes organized, readable code
indicators.description: Structures code clearly with sensible naming and formatting.

indicators.name: Tests program output
indicators.description: Runs the program and checks whether the output matches what was expected.

indicators.name: Debugs errors
indicators.description: Identifies the cause of an error and applies a fix to correct it.

name: 3. Digital Creativity & Project Development
description: The learner applies acquired technical skills to design and develop complete digital projects such as games, animations, or simple applications. They demonstrate creativity in idea generation, effective use of tools and features, and the ability to integrate multiple concepts into a coherent and functional solution.

indicators.name: Generates original project ideas
indicators.description: Proposes creative concepts for a game, animation, or application.

indicators.name: Uses tools and features effectively
indicators.description: Applies the relevant software or platform features to build the project.

indicators.name: Integrates multiple concepts
indicators.description: Combines several skills or components into one coherent, functional project.

indicators.name: Completes a functional project
indicators.description: Delivers a finished project that works as intended.

name: 4. AI & Data Literacy Awareness
description: The learner demonstrates an understanding of foundational concepts in artificial intelligence and data usage, including how systems process inputs and generate outputs. They can interact with basic AI tools, recognize the role of data in training systems, and show awareness of key issues such as bias, accuracy, and responsible use of AI technologies.

indicators.name: Explains how AI systems work
indicators.description: Describes, in simple terms, how an AI system takes inputs and produces outputs.

indicators.name: Interacts with AI tools
indicators.description: Uses a basic AI tool appropriately to complete a task.

indicators.name: Recognizes the role of data
indicators.description: Explains why data is needed to train an AI system.

indicators.name: Identifies bias and responsible use
indicators.description: Points out potential bias, accuracy issues, or ethical concerns in AI use.

name: 5. Collaboration, Communication & Digital Citizenship
description: The learner effectively collaborates with peers in shared tasks, clearly communicates ideas, processes, and outcomes, and engages constructively in discussions. They also demonstrate responsible digital behavior, including respect for others, safe use of technology, and adherence to ethical standards in online and collaborative environments.

indicators.name: Collaborates on shared tasks
indicators.description: Works effectively with peers to complete a group task.

indicators.name: Communicates ideas clearly
indicators.description: Explains ideas, processes, and outcomes so others can understand them.

indicators.name: Engages constructively in discussion
indicators.description: Contributes to discussions respectfully and takes others input into account.

indicators.name: Practices responsible digital behavior
indicators.description: Demonstrates safe, respectful, and ethical use of technology online.

name: Computational Thinking
description: The ability to break down problems and design step-by-step digital solutions.

indicators.name: Sequencing
indicators.description: Orders steps logically to complete a task.

indicators.name: Pattern Recognition
indicators.description: Identifies similarities/repetition to simplify problems.

indicators.name: Decomposition
indicators.description: Breaks a complex problem into smaller parts.

indicators.name: Abstraction
indicators.description: Filters out unnecessary detail to focus on what matters.

name: Digital Citizenship
description: Safe, ethical, and responsible use of technology.

indicators.name: Online Safety
indicators.description: Recognizes and avoids online risks.

indicators.name: Ethical Use of Technology
indicators.description: Uses digital tools and content responsibly.

indicators.name: Digital Communication
indicators.description: Communicates respectfully and clearly online.

name: Creativity & Problem Solving
description: Generating and refining original ideas to solve real problems.

indicators.name: Idea Generation
indicators.description: Produces multiple possible solutions to a problem.

indicators.name: Prototyping & Iteration
indicators.description: Builds, tests, and improves a solution.

name: Computational Problem-Solving
description: Applies logical, step-by-step thinking to break down and solve problems.
